name: First Vision Bank Of Tennessee
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank in Tennessee offering personal/business checking, savings, mortgages, and personal loans through four local branch locations.
description_long:
First Vision Bank is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank operating in Tennessee with physical locations in Tullahoma, Murfreesboro, Decherd, and Manchester. The bank emphasizes relationship-based banking and personalized service, positioning itself around the motto "Your Vision. Our Mission." Founded with a commitment to active community involvement, First Vision Bank serves both personal and business customers throughout middle Tennessee.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including personal and business checking accounts, savings accounts with Kasasa® rewards options, home mortgage products through First Vision Mortgage (purchase, refinance, and home equity options), and personal loans for vehicles, boats, farm equipment, and other purposes. They provide online banking, mobile banking, merchant services, cash management, and payroll services. The mortgage division operates from their Tullahoma headquarters with extended business hours (8 a.m.–5 p.m. weekdays).

First Vision Bank differentiates itself through emphasis on personalized relationship banking rather than one-size-fits-all solutions. The bank highlights that banking professionals personally meet with customers to tailor solutions to individual needs. They maintain Saturday drive-thru hours at their Tullahoma and Decherd locations, providing weekend access. The bank is a Federal Reserve System member and maintains NMLS# 879526.

As a regional community bank, First Vision Bank offers traditional banking services with local decision-making and branch accessibility. However, the website provides limited information about specific rates, fees, account minimums, or competitive product details. Customers seeking transparent rate comparisons or specialized digital-banking experiences may need to contact branches directly for detailed pricing information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Kasasa® rewards checking and savings accounts
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with backing by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Multiple Tennessee branch locations (Tullahoma, Murfreesboro, Decherd, Manchester) with extended Saturday drive-thru hours at Tullahoma and Decherd
Kasasa® rewards account options available for checking and savings
Full-service mortgage division (First Vision Mortgage) with dedicated staff and extended weekday hours
Personal loans available for diverse purposes including vehicles, boats, and farm equipment
Online and mobile banking platforms available for account management
Federal Reserve System member with established regulatory compliance and transparency
Strong community banking focus with dedicated professionals and personalized service approach
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to four Tennessee locations; not accessible to customers outside middle Tennessee
Website lacks specific information about interest rates, APRs, account fees, or minimum balance requirements
No mention of specialized services like investment advisory, wealth management, or business banking tools that larger banks offer
After-hours customer service requires calling 1-800-500-1044 rather than 24/7 support
Minimal digital innovation mentioned; no details about advanced fintech features or standalone mobile app capabilities
best_for: Middle Tennessee residents seeking community-focused banking with local branch relationships and personalized service
Small business owners in Tennessee requiring both business banking and access to mortgage products through the same institution
Homebuyers and homeowners in Tennessee interested in purchasing, refinancing, or accessing home equity through a local lender
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and Federal Reserve membership with in-person banking convenience over digital-first banking
diagnosis: First Vision Bank is best for middle Tennessee residents and business owners who value relationship-based community banking and have access to one of the four branch locations. The main caveat is limited geographic availability and lack of transparent pricing information on the website—customers must contact branches directly for rates, fees, and specific product terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
